channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Multitenant data management with TiDB
item.description: In a previous article, we explored various approaches to managing data for multitenant applications. We examined the benefits, compromises and limitations of different deployment models, including share-nothing, share-everything, and hybrid.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Joby conducts first FAA testing under Type Inspection Authorization in simulator in Marina
item.description: Type Inspection Authorization testing is considered the final phase of the type certification process, which paves the way for an aircraft to begin commercial passenger operations, according to Joby.
item.dc:creator: James Herrera, The Mercury News

item.title: Some Meta Quest headsets are being bricked by a software update – but a fix is rolling out
item.description: The latest v71 and v72 software updates have been locking users out of their devices over the holidays.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Musk calls some MAGA supporters ‘contemptible fools’ as visa row intensifies
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk labeled a section of President-elect Trump supporters as “contemptible fools” as the online debate around visas for highly skilled workers on the right intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Lawmakers, former officials weigh in on TikTok ban as case heads to Supreme Court
item.description: Top lawmakers, First Amendment advocacy groups and President-elect Trump weighed in Friday on a law that could ban TikTok in the U.S., as the Supreme Court prepares to take up the case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, The Hill

item.title: Scammers are targeting teens with these nasty tricks
item.description: Cybercriminals have more ways than ever to target teenagers; here are some of the most widespread scams and tricks to watch in order to keep your loved ones safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York state government to monitor its use of AI under a new law
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York state government agencies will have to conduct reviews and publish reports that detail how they're using artificial intelligence software, under a new law signed by Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: An online debate over foreign workers in tech shows tensions in Trump's political coalition
item.description: An online spat between factions of Donald Trump’s supporters over immigration and the tech industry has thrown internal divisions in the president-elect’s political movement into public display.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A 9th telecoms firm has been hit by a massive Chinese espionage campaign, the White House says
item.description: A top White House official says a ninth U.S. telecoms firm has been confirmed to have been hacked as part of a sprawling Chinese espionage campaign that gave officials in Beijing access to private texts and phone conversations of an unknown number of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lincoln returns to port after proving out ‘game changing’ connectivity
item.description: On its latest deployment, the Nimitz-class carrier went from slow military satellite uplinks to modern capabilities that move terabytes of data each day. The post Lincoln returns to port after proving out ‘game changing’ connectivity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: Mexico tests cellphone app allowing migrants to send alert if they are about to be detained in US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico is developing a cellphone app that will allow migrants to warn relatives and local consulates if they think they are about to be detained by the U.S. immigration department, a senior official said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bay Area parent sues California company for selling illegal e-bikes
item.description: A Fairfax resident has filed a lawsuit against an electric-bicycle manufacturer on allegations it sells vehicles that are illegal under California law.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Macdonald, The Mercury News

item.title: The Special Olympics embarks on digital journey to empower its athletes
item.description: The Special Olympics is overhauling its technology stack as part of a digital transformation designed to bolster the training of its athletes across the globe.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AMD in 2024: year in review
item.description: AMD had something of a mixed year, but more was happening with its CPUs compared to the firm’s graphics cards.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cómo trabaja Ikea con la IA: 30.000 empleados formados
item.description: Como la mayoría de las empresas, Ikea se centra en los datos y la inteligencia artificial (IA) para cumplir sus ambiciones de futuro. Por ello, hace cuatro años, la empresa desarrolló una estrategia de datos para todo el grupo, que se ha seguido desde entonces.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New 2025 laws hit hot topics from AI in movies to rapid-fire guns
item.description: New laws taking effect with the new year will affect Hollywood actors, social media stars and chatty motorists.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New European retail gig work app trend may influence US market
item.description: Stores such as Urban Outfitters, Lush and Uniqlo are recruiting gig workers in the U.K. to bulk up their stores.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Delivering efficiencies through automated software testing
item.description: Learn how innovative testing strategies can empower your team to deliver faster, better and more reliable software. The post Delivering efficiencies through automated software testing first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Roy, Cruz named most active X posters in Congress: Report
item.description: Rep. Chip Roy (R-Texas) and Sen. Ted Cruz (R-Texas) were named the most active posters on the social platform X this year among all lawmakers in Congress, according to a recent report. The Dec.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy defend Silicon Valley's foreign-born hires
item.description: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy, co-chairs of President-elect Trump’s new “Department of Government Efficiency,” are defending the tech industry’s reliance on foreign-born engineers as the incoming Trump administration prepares to crack down on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Network problems delay flights at two oneworld Alliance airlines
item.description: IT problems struck flights at two oneworld Alliance member airlines this week, leading to delays and flight cancellations. Network equipment connecting internal and external systems at Japan Airlines (JAL) malfunctioned early on Dec.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What is an IT consultant? Roles, types, salaries, and how to become one
item.description: The IT function within organizations has become far more complex in recent years. Enterprise infrastructures have expanded far beyond the traditional ones focused on company-owned and -operated data centers.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Japan Airlines was hit by a cyberattack, delaying flights during the year-end holiday season
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan Airlines said it was hit by a cyberattack Thursday, causing delays to more than 20 domestic flights but the carrier said it was able to stop the onslaught and restore its systems hours later. There was no impact on flight safety, it said.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Undersea power cable linking Finland and Estonia hit by outage, prompting investigation
item.description: FRANKFURT, Germany (AP) — A power cable linking Finland and Estonia under the Baltic Sea suffered an outage, prompting an investigation, Finnish Prime Minister Petteri Orpo said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Snowy owl rescued from car grille by Minnesota woman who saved another bird hours earlier
item.description: A great gray owl and a snowy owl are being treated by experts after being rescued by a northern Minnesota woman.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Timeshare terror leaves retired couple $50K in the hole after being scammed
item.description: The FBI is warning timeshare owners of a telemarketing scam tied to a Mexican drug cartel. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides steps to stay safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ford to donate $1M, fleet of vehicles to Trump inauguration
item.description: Ford Motor Co. said it will donate $1 million and a small fleet of vehicles for use in President-elect Trump’s inauguration, multiple outlets reported Monday. Ford joins a recent wave of major tech companies that have pledged hefty donations to the incoming president’s inaugural fund.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill
